content: Featured Video
Featured Video

US intelligence assessments, cited by The New York Times, indicate that Iran has been rapidly restoring underground missile bunkers and silos after they are struck, allowing launchers to return to operation within hours. Despite weeks of sustained US and Israeli attacks, Iran has retained a significant portion of its missile arsenal and continues to carry out strikes, adapting its tactics to preserve operational capacity. At the same time, US officials acknowledge growing uncertainty over the effectiveness of the assault. Extensive use of decoys, combined with launchers concealed in bunkers and caves, has made it difficult to determine how many targets destroyed were real. While Washington has claimed substantial progress, intelligence agencies have not been able to provide a confident estimate of how many launchers remain. Although the rate of Iranian missile fire has decreased, officials say this reflects a more calculated and restrained strategy rather than a collapse in capability. By conserving launchers, dispersing assets, and rapidly repairing damaged infrastructure, Iran appears to be maintaining its ability to sustain pressure over time, raising questions about how close the US is to achieving its stated objective of dismantling Iran’s missile program.
headline: Iran repairs missile bunkers within hours as US struggles to verify destroyed launchers

Senior SDF Military Source:

📌 28 SDF officers will travel to Damascus within three days to join the Defense Ministry’s military academy, including 18 from Al-Jazira and 10 from Kobane.

📌 They form the first batch under the Jan. 29, 2026 agreement. Training lasts 6–12 months to prepare them for brigade and battalion command, after which they will receive ranks including colonel and brigadier.

📌 Additional batches will be sent later as company-level commanders.

📌 The Jan. 29, 2026 agreement covers phased SDF integration with the Syrian army, formation of three brigades plus a Kobane battalion, civil administration integration, and protection of Kurdish rights and displaced persons’ return.

📌 The agreement aims to unify Syrian territories and fully integrate the region.

Source: Rudaw

As rocket fire from Hezbollah intensifies, Israeli officials increasingly acknowledge that the Lebanese resistance movement may be preparing for a sustained campaign along the northern front. According to a source cited by The Jerusalem Post, Israeli assessments suggest that the group’s operations are not a short-term escalation but part of a longer confrontation aimed at exhausting Israeli forces and reshaping the battlefield dynamics along the border.

Over recent days, Israeli sites in the north have remained under persistent pressure. Anti-tank missiles have repeatedly targeted Israeli forces’ positions near the frontier, reinforcing the sense within Israeli military and diplomatic circles that Hezbollah is deliberately maintaining a steady tempo of attacks. Some Israeli officials have questioned the rationale behind the campaign, portraying it as irrational—an argument frequently invoked by Tel Aviv whenever resistance movements challenge Israel's military posture.

Meanwhile, Lebanese officials say they are pursuing so-called 'diplomatic channels' to prevent further escalation. Washington, however, is reportedly dissatisfied with the approach of the Lebanese government, accusing them of failing to curb Hezbollah’s actions. According to the same source, the US administration has effectively dismissed the Lebanese proposal for now, and Hezbollah has shown no signs that they will pull back on their resistance operations.
headline: Hezbollah signals readiness for prolonged confrontation as pressure mounts on the northern front

📌 Reports confirm the extrajudicial killing of 21 prisoners in the Kobane area in Aleppo countryside between January 19 and 22, 2026. 

📌 The killings occurred at a detention center in Yedi Qawi village, under Syrian Democratic Forces control, following protests over unequal amnesty application and a mass prisoner escape. 

📌 Victims have been verified as civilians, not combatants; the documented death toll stands at 21, with estimates suggesting it could rise to 40–45 including those still missing. 

📌 The SDF acknowledged the circulated video but stated the deceased were government forces; detailed evidence analysis contradicts this claim. 

Source: Syrian Network for Human Rights

![Featured Image](https://i.ibb.co/WvcK1jsm/IMG-20260223-191536-647.jpg)

Since the start of Ramadan, Israeli occupation forces have arrested more than 100 Palestinians across the West Bank, including women, children, and former prisoners, amid a declared escalation in arrest campaigns that are shielded by settler attacks and aimed at entrenching control, according to the Palestinian Prisoners’ Club.

The arrests have spanned most governorates, including Jerusalem, where many detainees are later barred from Al-Aqsa Mosque, while field interrogations, described as collective retaliation, have intensified alongside widespread reports of severe beatings, home raids and destruction, confiscation of property, hostage-taking of family members, use of detainees as human shields, field executions, and settlement expansion under the cover of security operations. 

The Prisoners' Club stressed in its statement that these measures represent a continuation of longstanding policies targeting Palestinian presence, with the key shift since the start of the genocidal war being the scale and intensity of abuses; since then, roughly 22,000 arrest cases have been documented in the West Bank.
headline: Five days into Ramadan: Over 100 Palestinians detained by Israel in escalating West Bank crackdown

The Secretary General of Hezbollah, Sheikh Naim Qassem, in a speech:

'The brazen attacks by the Zionist entity against our forces and against civilians, in a blatant and ongoing violation of the ceasefire agreement, cannot continue. 

For months, we have remained silent out of concern for Lebanon, to preserve the peace, and to give the Lebanese state the chance to deal with these transgressions, but they have not. In fact, the Lebanese government is partly to blame for the escalating attacks due to its unwillingness to pressure Israel.

We call upon the Lebanese Army to stop all its disarmament efforts against Hezbollah, and we warn the Lebanese government that continuing its pursuit of this policy would constitute a grave mistake.

Regarding the ongoing aggression; the enemy may inflict pain upon us, but we, too, can inflict pain upon them. We do not want war nor are we seeking it, but there is a big difference between defending against aggression and initiating war. 

In any case, the current situation cannot continue. As for when, how, and what developments might change this reality, we will let events on the field tell the story.'
